run_metadata.coverage_summary: Attempted the full source universe (Tier 1 government, Tier 2 broader public sector/GBE, Tier 3 private). Direct page retrieval remained fully blocked this run: WebFetch returned HTTP 403 for every external URL tested (tenders.gov.au ATM detail pages, buy.nsw, vendorpanel.com.au) AND for benign control hosts (example.com, wikipedia.org), and direct curl-through-proxy was rejected with a 403 CONNECT for all hosts. The proxy status endpoint recorded the tenders.gov.au failure as an upstream/policy CONNECT denial, so no tender detail page, listing page, RSS/API endpoint or specification document could be opened. All capture was therefore from WebSearch result titles/snippets, cross-checked across multiple independent queries and rejecting any candidate whose close date could not be corroborated. Consistent with prior runs, search summaries repeatedly recycled already-closed opportunities as if current; every candidate was date-verified. Five buyer-originated Australian opportunities were confirmed current and enriched with verified closing dates this run: Defence AZ6626/ESTS4000PH6 Hobart Class Destroyer Aegis facilities EOI (published 6 Jul 2026, closes 17 Aug 2026), Clean Energy Regulator PIN_2026_0334 ICT-capability REOI (closes 10 Aug 2026 - newly confirmed, was headline-only last run), Defence LCD-26-0030 Army Innovation Day 2026 RFI (closes 27 Jul 2026 - newly confirmed close date, closing-soon), DCCEEW ATM_2026_6066 Strategic Property Purchases (closes 3 Aug 2026 - newly confirmed), and the Transport for NSW Mittagong steel-safety-barrier RFT (scope corroborated, close date still unrecoverable behind the 403 wall). One genuinely new buyer-originated source was recorded (Port of Newcastle e-tendering). Discarded as stale/closed after verification: PR10031930 RASS (closed 6 May 2026), MSD/RFT/55619/1 (closed 12 May 2025), ATM_2026_6026 solar-panel-recycling pilot (closed 24 Apr 2026), ASCA-PITCHDAY-26-DDC RFI (submissions closed 9 Jun 2026), and the NSW Health Far West LHD air-transport tender (closed 16 Jul 2026). Zero tenders were fabricated.
gaps: Direct page retrieval fully blocked again this run. WebFetch returned HTTP 403 for every external URL, including control hosts example.com and en.wikipedia.org, and direct curl-through-proxy was rejected with '403 CONNECT tunnel failed' for all hosts. The proxy status endpoint logged the tenders.gov.au attempt as a gateway 403 to CONNECT (policy denial or upstream failure). Consequence: no ATM/RFT detail page, no listing page, no RSS/API feed and no specification document could be opened; all detail is limited to cross-verified WebSearch snippets. Deliverables/requirements extraction from source documents was not possible this run.

Transport for NSW Mittagong steel-safety-barrier RFT: scope (inspection, repair and replacement of damaged guardrail and wire-rope steel safety barriers within the Mittagong road network) corroborated across two buy.nsw snippets, but no RFT ID and no closing date could be recovered (detail page 403). Recorded at headline level, pending a detail pass.

DCCEEW ATM_2026_6066 'Strategic Property Purchases': close date (3 Aug 2026 2:00pm ACT) and agency confirmed via search, but the full scope/deliverables and the exact program (appears property-acquisition, possibly a bushland program) could not be read from the 403-blocked detail page. Recorded with verified headline fields, deliverables pending detail.

Search snippets again recycled closed/stale opportunities as if current. Confirmed CLOSED and NOT recorded: PR10031930 RASS Remote Air Services Subsidy (closed 6 May 2026); MSD/RFT/55619/1 Designer Support Contract Services Maritime West (closed 12 May 2025); ATM_2026_6026 National Solar Panel Recycling Pilot (closed 24 Apr 2026); ASCA-PITCHDAY-26-DDC RFI (RFI submission window 4 May - 9 Jun 2026 closed); NSW Health Far West LHD Air Transport and Remote Health Services (closed 16 Jul 2026, just past).

Tier-1 state/territory portals (buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders/supply.qld, Tenders WA, SA Tenders, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders): reachable only as portal landing pages via search; individual open tenders could not be drilled into or date-verified behind the 403 wall, so apart from the TfNSW Mittagong item no state opportunity could be confirmed as newly published in the last 7 days at a trustworthy level of detail. Tenders WA additionally advertised a planned platform outage Sat 25 Jul 2026 08:00-12:00.

Tier-3 corporate (SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network, plus BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue, Woodside, Santos, Coles, Woolworths, Qantas, banks, telcos) checked as required. No publicly-advertised Australian RFx was visible without a supplier login; corporate opportunities remain registration-gated or invitation-only, so none were captured as tenders. Sydney Water similarly confirmed its tenders run only through registration-gated SAP Ariba. The relevant corporate portals are already recorded in prior runs' discovered_sources.

Deferred to a future run with working page retrieval: several other current 2026 AusTender ATM IDs surfaced by reference in snippets (AACAP 2026-P04, OPC-2026-03, AGO24/25-006 HIPP CRQA panel subcategory, ATM_2026_6786, RFT 1001032028) but could not be titled/dated/scoped without opening the 403-blocked detail pages, so they were not recorded rather than risk fabrication.
recommended_seed_additions: Port of Newcastle Operations - E-Tendering (buyer-originated GBE port corporation; advertises all RFT/EOI/RFP/RFQ via its own TenderLink-hosted portal at portal.tenderlink.com/portofnewcastle, landing page https://pon.com.au/trade-and-business/tenders/; supplier registration required). Not previously in the known-source list.

tenders.title: Facilities to Support Hobart Class Destroyer (DDG) Aegis Combat System Enhancement - Head Contractor EOI
tenders.category: Building construction and support and maintenance and repair services
tenders.description: Two-stage Expression of Interest for the Head Contractor package of works to deliver facilities supporting the Hobart Class Destroyer (DDG) Aegis Combat System Enhancement capability project, delivered under a Defence Head Contract (HC-GMP).
tenders.deliverables_summary: The successful head contractor will design and construct new and refurbished training facilities and working accommodation to support the Hobart Class Destroyer Aegis Combat System Enhancement, principally at the Navy Training Systems Centre - Randwick Barracks, NSW. Selection is a two-stage process (an Invitation to Register Interest stage followed by this EOI).
key_deliverables: Modification and expansion of existing training facilities
New and refurbished working accommodation at Navy Training Systems Centre - Randwick Barracks, NSW
tenders.scope_of_work: Head Contractor package of works under Defence Head Contract HC-GMP for the ESTS4000PH6 project 'Facilities to Support the Hobart Class Destroyer Combat System Enhancement Capability Project'. Scope includes modified and expanded training facilities and working accommodation, including construction of new and refurbished training facilities at Navy Training Systems Centre - Randwick Barracks, NSW. Delivered via a two-staged selection process (Invitation to Register Interest / ITR as stage one, followed by this Expression of Interest).
tenders.eligibility: Open (two-stage; respondents to the EOI are drawn from the earlier Invitation to Register Interest stage)
tenders.detail_source: AusTender ATM search snippet (ATM ID AZ6626, title, agency, publish date, close date and location) cross-checked against project-scope snippet for ESTS4000PH6; detail page returned HTTP 403
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via AusTender ATM keyword search (Defence); corroborated across two independent WebSearch queries (AZ6626 ATM header fields and ESTS4000PH6 project scope). Buyer-originated on AusTender.
tenders.notes: ATM ID is AZ6626; ESTS4000PH6 is the Defence project/estate works code. Multi-stage EOI. Publish date and close date verified from search; full tender documents, evaluation criteria, value and contact could not be read (detail page 403).

tenders.title: Request for Expressions of Interest - ICT Capability to Support Regulatory and Registry Functions
tenders.description: Stage 1 Request for Expressions of Interest (REOI) seeking suppliers with relevant experience and capability to support the Clean Energy Regulator's current and future regulatory and registry functions through ICT capability.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The Clean Energy Regulator is running a two-stage procurement. This Stage 1 REOI is an information-gathering and shortlisting exercise: it seeks to understand market capability, solution approaches and implementation considerations for ICT capability supporting its regulatory and registry functions, and will be used to shortlist respondents for a subsequent Stage 2 Proof of Concept (PoC) procurement.
key_deliverables: Response demonstrating ICT capability to support regulatory and registry functions
Description of solution approach and implementation considerations
Basis for shortlisting into Stage 2 Proof of Concept
tenders.scope_of_work: REOI (Stage 1 of a two-stage process) to identify and shortlist suppliers able to support the Clean Energy Regulator's current and future regulatory and registry functions via ICT capability. Objectives: information gathering on market capability/solution approaches/implementation considerations, and shortlisting respondents for a subsequent Proof of Concept procurement in Stage 2.
tenders.eligibility: Open to suppliers with relevant ICT experience and capability
tenders.detail_source: AusTender search snippet for PIN_2026_0334 (scope, two-stage structure and close date 10 Aug 2026 5:00pm ACT); detail page returned HTTP 403
tenders.reasoning: Re-verified from prior run (was headline-only). Closing date 10 Aug 2026 now confirmed via a dedicated WebSearch query. Buyer-originated on AusTender.
tenders.notes: Closing date and two-stage structure now confirmed (previously unverified). Publish date and value still not recoverable behind the 403 wall.

tenders.title: Army Innovation Day 2026 (AID26) - RFI for Autonomous Collaborative Systems for Contested Littoral Warfare
tenders.description: Request for Information for Army Innovation Day 2026 (AID26), themed 'Autonomous Mass in Littoral Warfare', seeking innovative technologies (Technology Readiness Level 4-6) for autonomous systems that operate collaboratively in contested littoral environments.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Respondents submit information on innovative, TRL 4-6 autonomous systems able to operate collaboratively in contested littoral environments. The most promising respondents will be invited to participate in AID26 experimentation and demonstration activities held at military establishments in the Adelaide region over 2-13 November 2026; invitations to participate are to be issued from 17 August 2026.
key_deliverables: RFI response describing a TRL 4-6 autonomous collaborative system for contested littoral operations
Basis for selection into AID26 experimentation/demonstration activities (Adelaide region, 2-13 Nov 2026)
tenders.scope_of_work: RFI seeking responses on innovative technologies for autonomous systems that operate collaboratively in contested littoral environments, reflecting National Defence Strategy 2026 priorities (adoption of autonomous systems, generation of mass at scale, operating effectively in contested littoral environments). Selected respondents participate in an Army-led experimentation and demonstration event at military establishments in the Adelaide region, 2-13 November 2026.
tenders.briefing: AID26 experimentation/demonstration event to be held at military establishments in the Adelaide region, 2-13 November 2026 (participation by invitation, issued from 17 August 2026)
tenders.submission_method: Lodge electronically via AusTender using the Lodgement Page link on the tender notification
tenders.detail_source: AusTender search snippets for LCD-26-0030 (RFI theme/scope, TRL 4-6, close date 27 Jul 2026 10:00am ACT, event dates, invitation timing); detail page returned HTTP 403
tenders.reasoning: Re-verified from prior run; closing date 27 Jul 2026 now confirmed via dedicated WebSearch query. Buyer-originated on AusTender (managed by Department of Defence - DSRG).
tenders.notes: Closing date now confirmed - closing soon (27 Jul 2026). RFI (market sounding / innovation call), not a contract award. Full documents and contact not recoverable behind 403 wall.

tenders.title: Expression of Interest for Strategic Property Purchases
tenders.buyer: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water
tenders.description: Approach to market for strategic property purchases by the Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water.
tenders.detail_source: AusTender search snippet for ATM_2026_6066 (title, agency, close date 3 Aug 2026 2:00pm ACT); detail page returned HTTP 403
tenders.reasoning: Re-verified from prior run; agency and closing date confirmed via dedicated WebSearch query. Buyer-originated on AusTender.
tenders.notes: Closing date and buying agency confirmed this run. Full scope/deliverables not recoverable behind the 403 wall - retained at headline level pending a detail pass. Appears to relate to strategic/bushland property acquisition.

tenders.title: Inspection, Repair and Replacement of Steel Safety Barriers - Mittagong Road Network
tenders.description: Request for Tender by Transport for NSW for the inspection, repair and replacement of damaged steel safety barriers, including guardrail and wire rope safety barrier systems, within the Mittagong road network.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The supplier must inspect, repair and replace damaged steel safety barriers - including guardrail and wire rope safety barrier systems - within the Mittagong road network in the NSW Southern Highlands.
key_deliverables: Inspection of steel safety barriers within the Mittagong road network
Repair of damaged guardrail and wire rope safety barrier systems
Replacement of damaged steel safety barriers
tenders.scope_of_work: The works comprise the inspection, repair and replacement of damaged steel safety barriers, including guardrail and wire rope safety barrier systems, within the Mittagong road network.
tenders.delivery_location: Mittagong road network, Southern Highlands, NSW
tenders.detail_source: buy.nsw / Transport for NSW search snippets (scope description); no RFT ID, publish date or close date recoverable (detail page 403)
tenders.reasoning: Re-verified from prior run. Scope corroborated across two buy.nsw snippets, but the RFT ID and closing date remain unrecoverable behind the 403 wall. Buyer-originated (Transport for NSW on buy.nsw).
tenders.notes: Headline-level only. RFT ID, publish date and closing date could not be confirmed - do NOT rely on any specific close date until a detail pass succeeds. Retained because scope is well corroborated and the opportunity appears current.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier-2 GBE source-discovery search for port corporations' procurement channels. Port of Newcastle advertises all its RFT, EOI, RFP and RFQ via its own TenderLink-hosted e-tendering portal (portal.tenderlink.com/portofnewcastle); suppliers must register to view/respond. This is the buyer's own channel, not a paywalled aggregator.
discovered_sources.notes: Landing page https://pon.com.au/trade-and-business/tenders/; portal https://portal.tenderlink.com/portofnewcastle/alltenders/. New vendors complete a Vendor Request Form. Not previously in the known-source list. (Port Authority of NSW, WaterNSW and ICN Gateway were also probed but are already known sources.)

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Known canonical Tier-1 source; primary yield this run (all four Commonwealth opportunities above). Reachable only via WebSearch snippets - detail pages returned HTTP 403.
discovered_sources.notes: ATM detail pages (tenders.gov.au/Atm/Show/...) consistently returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch this run; only search snippets were usable.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Known canonical Tier-1 source; probed this run (TfNSW Mittagong item). eTendering has migrated into the buy.nsw Tenders module tied to Supplier Hub.
discovered_sources.notes: Detail pages returned HTTP 403; only landing/search snippets usable.

discovered_sources.source_name: SAP Business Network Discovery (SAP Ariba Discovery)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier-3 check. No publicly-advertised Australian RFx was visible without a supplier login this run; corporate opportunities remain registration-gated or invitation-only.
discovered_sources.notes: Free supplier registration required to view full opportunity detail. Recorded as the portal per the visibility rules; no gated opportunities were invented.
